featuredBeliefs: Accessible law is a form of equity.
Rights that vary by state aren't rights — they're privileges.
gridBeliefs: The words in laws determine who gets protected.
Policy change starts in networks, not legislatures.
Proximity to power is not proximity to truth.
Durable reform requires organizing and drafting to move together.

process.title: Define the Question
process.desc: What does this policy actually do — to whom, and why?

process.title: Follow the Evidence
process.desc: Text, data, and the people inside the system.

process.title: Surface the Pattern
process.desc: Where does power move, and what does it leave behind?

process.title: Translate for Impact
process.desc: Findings that reach the people who can act on them.
